relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The people rise from Shittim and wait beside the river they have never crossed.
Three days of waiting end when officers move through the camp.
The ark becomes the visible sign of a path opening ahead.
Distance protects the sacred and lets everyone see where to go.
The people prepare themselves for wonder without pretending they control it.
Priests lift the ark and enter the way before the people.
Joshua's leadership is publicly confirmed through continuity of presence.
The priests must stand inside the water before the crossing opens.
The people are called close enough to hear the word.
The sign is interpreted within the dangerous promise of dispossessing other peoples.
The ark of the Lord of all earth enters the river first.
Every tribe contributes one witness to the memory about to be made.
Feet touch the flood, and the descending water is pictured gathering into a heap.
The camp becomes a moving people behind the ark.
The priests enter a Jordan swollen beyond its banks.
The river opens between Adam and the Salt Sea, and the people cross opposite Jericho.
The priests remain in the exposed center until every person reaches dry ground.

contemplative_note: Joshua 3 is a threshold story: feet enter before the path is visible, sacred presence stays in the dangerous middle, and no one is left crossing alone. Its miracle must not be recruited to bless the dispossession attached to it.
